content.cn_text: 2. 您将听到米德尔敦城市农业计划的主席向一群人讲话
content.en_text: 2. You will hear the chairperson of the Middletown Urban Farming scheme talking to a group

content.cn_text: 对加入该计划感兴趣的人。
content.en_text: of people who are interested in joining the scheme.

content.cn_text: 首先，您有时间查看第3页上的问题11-16。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 11-16 on page 3.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答问题11-16。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11-16.

content.cn_text: 我是克里斯·巴特勒，我是米德尔敦城市农业计划（Middletown Urban Farming scheme）的主席，简称
content.en_text: I'm Chris Butler, and I'm the chairperson of the Middletown Urban Farming scheme, or

content.cn_text: MUFS，一个非常成功的项目，鼓励城市居民种植自己的
content.en_text: MUFS for short, a highly successful program which encourages city dwellers to grow their

content.cn_text: 水果和蔬菜，如果您愿意，可以成为城市农夫。
content.en_text: own fruit and vegetables to become city farmers if you will.

content.cn_text: 谁创立了MUFS？
content.en_text: Who started MUFS?

content.cn_text: 嗯，城市农业的想法已经存在相当长一段时间了，特别是在城市规划者中。
content.en_text: Well, the idea of urban farming has been around for quite a while, particularly among town
planners.

content.cn_text: 但实际上是包括我在内的一群米德尔敦商人决定
content.en_text: But it was actually a group of Middletown business people, including myself, who decided

content.cn_text: 创建了后来的MUFS。
content.en_text: to create what became MUFS.

content.cn_text: 大学农业专家是后来才参与进来的。
content.en_text: University agriculture specialists got involved later.

content.cn_text: 最初，我们的目标不是为了实现减少因食物运输而产生的二氧化碳排放量的环保目标，
content.en_text: Now, initially, the aim was not some eco-friendly goal of reducing CO2 emissions caused by the

content.cn_text: 即从农场到餐桌的食物运输。
content.en_text: transport of food from farm to dinner plate.

content.cn_text: 而是为了最大限度地利用城市内的空置或闲置土地。
content.en_text: It was simply to maximize the utilization of the vacant or idle land that lies within
the city.

content.cn_text: 当然，后来我们意识到，一个额外的好处可能是当地人更健康的饮食。
content.en_text: Of course, later we realized that an additional benefit might be a healthier diet for local
people.

content.cn_text: 让我告诉你们一些已经加入该计划的团体的情况。
content.en_text: Let me tell you a bit about the groups that have joined the scheme.

content.cn_text: 我们已经有许多学校和大量公司员工参与其中，所以我们的信息
content.en_text: We've got any number of schools involved and loads of corporate employees, so our message

content.cn_text: 似乎在那里受到了很好的反响。
content.en_text: seems to have been well received there.

content.cn_text: 但明年，我们将集中精力吸引各种社区中心加入，例如
content.en_text: But for next year, we're going to concentrate on getting various community centers, like

content.cn_text: 老年人活动中心，因为到目前为止，他们对参与该计划一直有些迟缓。
content.en_text: those for senior citizens to join, because so far, they've been somewhat slow to take
up the scheme.

content.cn_text: 现在，MUFS的核心委员会是一群专注的园艺爱好者。
content.en_text: Now, the core committee of the MUFS is a group of dedicated gardeners.

content.cn_text: 他们可能不熟悉您具体的地块。
content.en_text: They probably won't be familiar with your specific plot of land.

content.cn_text: 他们大多是业余爱好者。
content.en_text: They're mostly amateurs.

content.cn_text: 但他们是懂园艺门道的人，他们很乐意
content.en_text: But they are the people who know the ins and outs of gardening, and they will be happy

content.cn_text: 与任何MUFS成员坐下来，就园艺提供一些实践性建议。
content.en_text: to sit down with any MUFS member and make some hands-on suggestions about gardening.

content.cn_text: 但不要指望他们能回答技术问题，比如植物的拉丁名称。
content.en_text: But don't expect them to know the answers to technical questions, like the Latin names
of plants.

content.cn_text: MUFS热衷于支持身体残疾的成员。
content.en_text: The MUFS are keen to support members who are physically disabled.

content.cn_text: 他们正在考虑为无法使用普通工具的成员提供专门改装的园艺工具供借用
content.en_text: They're looking at the possibility of providing specially adapted gardening tools on loan
to members who can't cope with regular tools.

content.cn_text: 但这还没有实现。
content.en_text: That's not happening yet.

content.cn_text: 但我们确实有一些窗台花箱和更大的容器，我们可以免费分发给这些成员，
content.en_text: But we do have some window boxes, as well as larger containers, which we can distribute

content.cn_text: 尽管他们必须自己提供土壤和
content.en_text: free of charge to these members, although they will have to provide their own soil and

content.cn_text: 堆肥。
content.en_text: compost to themselves.

content.cn_text: 我尤其为我们与当地学校（包括小学和中学）所做的工作感到自豪。
content.en_text: I'm especially proud of the work we've done with the local schools, both grade school
level and high schools.

content.cn_text: 老师们说，他们知道这个计划在生物等科学课上会有一些实际用途
content.en_text: The teachers say that they knew the scheme would be of some practical use in science classes
like biology.

content.cn_text: 尽管有些人最初抱有希望，但这些果蔬地块并未为学生提供更便宜的
content.en_text: Despite the original hopes of some, the fruit and vegetable plots haven't provided cheaper

content.cn_text: 午餐。
content.en_text: lunches for their students.

content.cn_text: 但他们没有预料到的是，这个计划在学生中带来了态度上的改变。
content.en_text: But what they didn't anticipate was the change in attitude that the scheme has created
amongst their students.

content.cn_text: 他们说学生们对周围的环境变得更加关注。
content.en_text: They say the students have become much more conscious of their surrounding environment.

content.cn_text: 他们希望保持环境整洁有序。
content.en_text: They want to keep it neat and tidy.

content.cn_text: 在听讲座的其余部分之前，您有一些时间查看第4页上的问题17-20。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the talk, you have some time to look at questions 17-20 on
page 4.

content.cn_text: 现在请听并回答问题17-20。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 17-20.

content.cn_text: 现在，许多当地组织和企业为我们的会员提供免费商品和服务。
content.en_text: Now a number of local organizations and businesses provide our members with free goods and services.

content.cn_text: 我来介绍其中一些。
content.en_text: Let me go through some of them.

content.cn_text: 市医院，正如您所料，需要为其病人提供数千份餐食。
content.en_text: The city hospital, as you might expect, has to provide thousands of meals for its patients.

content.cn_text: 直到其厨房经理为我们的会员开设了一门课程，教授如何使用蔬菜
content.en_text: Until the manager of its kitchen runs a course for our members on how to use vegetables

content.cn_text: 制作各种菜肴。
content.en_text: in a huge variety of dishes.

content.cn_text: 地方政府发现他们有一个大型玻璃建筑，公园部门曾用它来
content.en_text: The local government found it had a large glass building, which the Parks Department used,

content.cn_text: 在冬季培育花卉种子和幼苗。
content.en_text: to start off flower seeds in young plants during the winter.

content.cn_text: 他们同意允许我们的会员免费使用这个地方来培育他们自己的小
content.en_text: And they agreed to allow our members to use this free of charge to get their own little

content.cn_text: 幼苗，使其有一个良好的开端。
content.en_text: seedlings off to a good start.

content.cn_text: 还有一件事。
content.en_text: Another thing.

content.cn_text: 大学同意，如果会员寄送土壤样本给他们，他们将
content.en_text: The university has agreed that if members send them a sample of their soil, they will

content.cn_text: 对土壤进行一系列测试，以指出哪种植物最适合生长以及最好使用哪种类型的
content.en_text: do a series of tests on it to indicate what kind of plants will grow best and what types

content.cn_text: 肥料和营养物质。
content.en_text: of fertilizer and nutrients would be best to use.

content.cn_text: 为了确保我们的会员知道什么对他们有益，一家超市正在提供一系列
content.en_text: And just to make sure our members know what's good for them, a supermarket is offering a

content.cn_text: 关于如何通过合理饮食来减肥和保持健康的课程。
content.en_text: series of classes on how to lose weight and stay healthy by having a sensible diet.

content.cn_text: 现在，我们来谈谈实际操作。
content.en_text: Now, let's talk about the practicalities.

content.cn_text: 第二部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 2.

content.cn_text: 现在您有半分钟时间检查答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 现在翻到第5页的第三部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 3 on page 5.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 11-16.

items.title: Who founded the Middletown Urban Farming Scheme?
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第11题询问MUFS的创始人是谁。原文第11-12行提到：“But it was actually a group of Middletown business people, including myself, who decided to create what became MUFS.”（但实际上是包括我在内的一群米德尔敦商人决定创建了后来的MUFS。），明确指出是商人创建的。因此选项B“business people”（商人）是正确答案。 原文第9-10行提到：“the idea of urban farming has been around for quite a while, particularly among town planners.”（城市农业的想法已经存在相当长一段时间了，特别是在城市规划者中。），这表明城市规划者有这个想法，但并不是MUFS的创始人，因此选项C是干扰项。 原文第13行提到：“University agriculture specialists got involved later.”（大学农业专家是后来才参与进来的。），明确指出大学专家是后来才参与的，并非创始人，因此选项A是干扰项。

items.title: What was the original reason for starting the MUFS?

options.title: A
options.content: to encourage more efficient land use

items.analysis: 答案解析： 第12题询问创办MUFS的最初原因。原文第16-17行提到：“It was simply to maximize the utilization of the vacant or idle land that lies within the city.”（它只是为了最大限度地利用城市内闲置的土地。），这与选项A“to encourage more efficient land use”（鼓励更有效地利用土地）相符。因此选项A是正确答案。 原文第14-15行提到：“initially, the aim was not some eco-friendly goal of reducing CO2 emissions...”(最初的目的并非是减少二氧化碳排放量的环保目标...), 明确指出这不是最初目的，因此选项B是干扰项。 原文第18-19行提到：“Of course, later we realized that an additional benefit might be a healthier diet for local people.”（当然，后来我们才意识到，一个额外的好处可能是当地人更健康的饮食。），“later”表明这并非最初原因，因此选项C是干扰项。

items.title: For the coming year, the MUFS will focus on recruiting members from
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第13题询问明年MUFS将重点招募哪些成员。原文第23-24行提到：“But for next year, we're going to concentrate on getting various community centers, like those for senior citizens to join...” （但明年，我们将集中精力吸引各种社区中心加入，例如老年人活动中心...），明确指出明年重点招募社区中心。因此选项C“community centres”（社区中心）是正确答案。 原文第21行提到：“We've got any number of schools involved and loads of corporate employees...” （我们已经有许多学校和大量公司员工参与其中...），表明学校和公司员工已经大量参与，因此选项A和B不是明年的重点招募对象，是干扰项。

items.title: What kind of information is available from members of the MUFS committee?
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第14题询问MUFS委员会成员可以提供哪类信息。原文第29-30行提到：“they will be happy to sit down with any MUFS member and make some hands-on suggestions about gardening.”（他们很乐意与任何MUFS成员坐下来，就园艺提供一些实践性建议。），这与选项A“practical gardening tips”（实用园艺技巧）相符。因此选项A是正确答案。 原文第31-32行提到：“But don't expect them to know the answers to technical questions, like the Latin names of plants.”（但不要指望他们能回答技术问题，比如植物的拉丁名称。），这表明他们不提供植物科学方面的知识，因此选项B是干扰项。 原文第27行提到：“They probably won't be familiar with your specific plot of land.”（他们可能不熟悉您具体的地块。），这表明他们无法提供当地园艺地点的具体信息，因此选项C是干扰项。

items.title: What are provided by the MUFS for the physically disabled?
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第15题询问MUFS为身体残疾者提供什么。原文第37-38行提到：“But we do have some window boxes, as well as larger containers, which we can distribute free of charge to these members...” （但我们确实有一些窗台花箱和更大的容器，我们可以免费分发给这些成员...），这与选项B“containers for plants”（植物容器）相符。因此选项B是正确答案。 原文第38-39行提到：“although they will have to provide their own soil and compost to themselves.”（尽管他们必须自己提供土壤和堆肥。），明确表示MUFS不提供土壤和堆肥，因此选项A是干扰项。 原文第34-36行提到：“They're looking at the possibility of providing specially adapted gardening tools on loan... That's not happening yet.”（他们正在考虑提供专门改装的园艺工具供借用...但这还没有实现。），明确指出园艺工具目前尚未提供，因此选项C是干扰项。

items.title: According to school teachers, what has been an unexpected advantage of the MUFS?

options.title: A
options.content: its usefulness as an aid for teaching science

options.title: B
options.content: its contribution to reducing costs of school meals

options.title: C
options.content: its help in encouraging community pride among students

items.analysis: 答案解析： 第16题询问根据学校老师的说法，MUFS的一个意想不到的好处是什么。原文第46-49行提到：“But what they didn't anticipate was the change in attitude that the scheme has created amongst their students. They say the students have become much more conscious of their surrounding environment. They want to keep it neat and tidy.”（但他们没有预料到的是，这个计划在学生中带来了态度上的改变。他们说学生们对周围的环境变得更加关注。他们希望保持环境整洁有序。），“neat and tidy”体现了学生对社区环境的责任感和自豪感，这与选项C“its help in encouraging community pride among students”（它有助于培养学生的社区自豪感）相符。因此选项C是正确答案。 原文第42-43行提到：“The teachers say that they knew the scheme would be of some practical use in science classes like biology.”（老师们说，他们知道这个计划在生物等科学课上会有一些实际用途。），这表明其在科学教学中的作用是老师们“knew”（知道）的，也就是预料到的，而非意想不到的，因此选项A是干扰项。 原文第44-45行提到：“Despite the original hopes of some, the fruit and vegetable plots haven't provided cheaper lunches for their students.”（尽管有些人最初抱有希望，但这些果蔬地块并未为学生提供更便宜的午餐。），明确指出没有降低学校餐费，因此选项B是干扰项。

groups.desc: Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter A–F, next to questions 17–20.
groups.title: Questions 17-20.

items.title: city hospital
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第17题询问市医院提供的免费服务。原文第56-57行提到：“Until the manager of its kitchen runs a course for our members on how to use vegetables in a huge variety of dishes.”（直到其厨房经理为我们的会员开设了一门课程，教授如何使用蔬菜制作各种菜肴。），这与选项B“cooking lessons”（烹饪课程）相符。因此选项B是正确答案。

items.title: local government
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第18题询问地方政府提供的免费服务。原文第58行提到：“The local government found it had a large glass building”（地方政府发现他们有一个大型玻璃建筑），随后第60-61行指出：“And they agreed to allow our members to use this free of charge to get their own little seedlings off to a good start.”（他们同意允许我们的会员免费使用这个地方来培育他们自己的小幼苗，使其有一个良好的开端。），一个用于培育幼苗的“大型玻璃建筑”就是温室（greenhouse）。这与选项E“use of a greenhouse”（使用温室）相符。因此选项E是正确答案。

items.title: university
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第19题询问大学提供的免费服务。原文第63-65行提到：“The university has agreed that if members send them a sample of their soil, they will do a series of tests on it to indicate what kind of plants will grow best and what types of fertilizer and nutrients would be best to use.”（大学同意，如果会员寄送土壤样本给他们，他们将对土壤进行一系列测试，以指出哪种植物最适合生长以及最好使用哪种类型的肥料和营养物质。），这与选项C“advice on soil”（土壤建议）相符。虽然也提到了肥料，但核心是通过土壤测试提供种植建议。因此选项C是正确答案。

items.title: supermarket
items.analysis: 答案解析： 第20题询问超市提供的免费服务。原文第66-67行提到：“a supermarket is offering a series of classes on how to lose weight and stay healthy by having a sensible diet.”（一家超市正在提供一系列关于如何通过合理饮食来减肥和保持健康的课程。），这与选项D“eat-to-keep-fit course”（饮食健身课程）相符。因此选项D是正确答案。

source_label: 🍠@Listening 永不虾滑 · 仅供个人学习
source_credit: 此内容由🍠@Listening 永不虾滑整理，仅作为分享供个人使用；禁止商用、倒卖和引流。有问题请先仔细看🍠这个账号的置顶和分享贴。没有团队，不是机构，纯个人分享。督学和其他相关课程请咨询主页另一个号@心情有点虾滑（不回任何机经相关消息）。
